task.title: Add Test Scripts to a Request
task.shortdesc: Write JavaScript test scripts in the Tests tab to validate API responses automatically after each request is sent.

prereq.p: Before you add test scripts, create a request and verify that it returns a response. For instructions, see .

context.p: Postman runs test scripts after every response is received. Tests use the pm.test() function combined with pm.expect() assertions from the Chai library. Each test has a name and an assertion. Postman reports pass or fail for each assertion in the Test Results tab of the response viewer.
You can write tests at the request level or at the collection and folder level. Collection-level and folder-level scripts run before or after every request in that group.

step.cmd: Open the saved request in the workbench.
Select the Tests tab in the request builder.
To insert a test quickly, select a snippet from the Snippets panel on the right side of the Tests tab.
step.stepresult: Postman inserts the snippet code into the test editor.

step.cmd: Modify the inserted snippet or write a custom test in the editor.

stepxmp.p: The following example tests verify the status code, content type, and a field value in the response body:

step.cmd: Select Send to run the request and execute the tests.
Select the Test Results tab in the response viewer to review the test outcomes.
step.stepresult: Each test displays a green PASS or red FAIL label with the test name. Failed tests include an error message explaining the assertion that was not met.

r.p: Postman runs the test scripts after the response is received and reports the results in the Test Results tab. Passing tests appear in green and failing tests appear in red.

postreq.p: After adding tests to individual requests, run the entire collection to execute all tests in sequence. For instructions, see Run a Collection.
